## C.L.E.A.R. - author's self-review

Answer each line yourself before requesting review. This front-loads the hard questions so review time is cheaper.

- **Context** - What problem motivated this PR? What would break or stay broken if we didn't ship it?
  >
- **Logic** - Walk through the control flow. What's the happy path? Is there a subtle branch that deserves attention from the reviewer?
  >
- **Edge cases** - List cases you tested or decided to ignore: empty input, concurrent calls, error recovery, large payloads, auth failures, network timeouts, cache hits, expired tokens. Name the ones that apply.
  >
- **Architecture** - Does this PR follow existing conventions (DI, error classes, schema validation, MUI usage, RSC boundary)? If it deviates, why? Any new public boundary to protect?
  >
- **Readability** - Will the next reader understand this without pinging you? Are names honest, comments purposeful, and commits atomic?
  >

## AI disclosure

<!-- Transparency about AI-assisted work. All OGStack work is Claude Code. This is about scope, not attribution. -->

- **Generated by Claude Code:** <!-- yes / partial / no -->
- **Skill(s) invoked:** <!-- e.g. /add-module, /add-page, /commit, /pr, or "none" -->
- **Human edits after generation:** <!-- short description, e.g. "adjusted pagination defaults plus wrote all tests by hand" -->
- **Review performed by a second human reviewer:** <!-- yes / no. PRs should have one. -->
